meta.name: Image Resizer
meta.description: Resize image dimensions directly in your browser and export the result as PNG, JPEG, or WebP.

workspaceTitle: Resize images locally, with visible tradeoffs instead of hidden uploads.
workspaceDescription: Choose an image, adjust width and height, decide whether to preserve the aspect ratio, and export a resized version without sending the file to a server.
contentTitle: What to expect from browser-local resizing
contentDescription: These notes explain which cases this tool handles well and where a heavier image pipeline is still the better choice.
uploadDescription: Pick a local image file to inspect its dimensions and generate a resized export.
uploadHint: PNG, JPEG, WebP, and other browser-readable formats are supported.
optionsDescription: Dimensions, interpolation style, output format, and quality are all controlled locally in the browser.
keepAspectRatioDescription: Changing width or height automatically adjusts the other side.
allowUpscaleDescription: Permit output dimensions larger than the source image.
qualityDescription: Used for lossy outputs such as JPEG and WebP.
resultDescription: Compare the original image with the resized output before downloading it.
emptyResultTitle: Resize an image to preview the output.
emptyResultDescription: The resized image and download action appear here after processing finishes.
invalidImageTitle: This file could not be read as an image.
invalidImageDescription: Choose a browser-readable image file and try again.
resizeErrorTitle: The image could not be resized.
resizeErrorDescription: Try a smaller output size or choose another format.
